skill.name: ralph
skill.description: Specification-first AI development powered by Ouroboros. Socratic questioning exposes hidden assumptions before writing code. Evolutionary loop (Interview → Seed → Execute → Evaluate → Evolve) runs until ontology converges. Ralph mode persists until verification passes — the boulder never stops. Use when user says "ralph", "ooo", "don't stop", "must complete", "until it works", "keep going", "interview me", or "stop prompting".
skill.summary: AI development methodology with Socratic questioning and persistent execution loops until verification passes
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate AI development methodology skill. Static findings for external_commands (194), network (6), and filesystem (28) are false positives - the script uses standard shell operations for CLI tool configuration, URLs are GitHub references in documentation, and hidden file access is for configuration files. No malicious intent detected.

content.user_title: Run persistent AI loops until verified
content.value_statement: Vague requirements and incomplete tasks waste development time. Ralph provides specification-first methodology with autonomous iteration until verification passes, ensuring tasks complete properly.
actual_capabilities: Socratic interviewing to expose hidden assumptions before coding
Crystallize requirements into immutable YAML specifications
Execute via Double Diamond decomposition methodology
3-stage evaluation gate: Mechanical, Semantic, and Consensus checks
Persistent Ralph loop that continues until verification passes
Drift detection to measure execution deviation from spec
limitations: Requires clear trigger keywords to activate (ooo, ralph, don't stop, etc.)
May generate excessive iterations for trivial tasks
Evaluation stages beyond Mechanical require additional setup
Not designed for one-off simple queries

use_cases.title: Complex feature development
use_cases.description: Use Socratic interviewing to clarify vague requirements, then execute with persistent verification loops until acceptance criteria are met.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers building features with unclear requirements

use_cases.title: Bug fixing with verification
use_cases.description: Run Ralph loop to persistently fix failing tests until all pass, with drift monitoring to ensure fixes align with original intent.
use_cases.target_user: Developers debugging and fixing code issues

use_cases.title: Specification clarification
use_cases.description: When requirements are ambiguous, use ooo interview to ask Socratic questions until ambiguity score is below threshold.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads and architects defining system requirements

prompt_templates.title: Basic Socratic interview
prompt_templates.prompt: ooo interview "I want to build a task management CLI"
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project with unclear requirements

prompt_templates.title: Generate specification seed
prompt_templates.scenario: After interview completes, crystallize into immutable spec

prompt_templates.title: Persistent task completion
prompt_templates.scenario: Persistent loop until verification passes - the boulder never stops

output_examples.input: ooo interview "I want to build a REST API"
output_examples.output: Socratic questions: What data will your API expose? Who are the consumers? What are the authentication requirements? What success looks like?
[Ralph Iteration 1/10] Verification: FAILED Score: 0.6 - Found 12 errors, analyzing... [Iteration 2/10] Verification: FAILED Score: 0.8 - 3 errors remain...

best_practices: Start with ooo interview for any vague requirements to reduce ambiguity before coding
Use the Ambiguity formula (Goal + Constraint + Success weighted scoring) to determine when ready for seed
Set appropriate max-iterations for Ralph mode based on task complexity
anti_patterns: Using Ralph mode for simple one-off queries that could be answered directly
Skipping the Socratic interview and going straight to execution with unclear requirements
Ignoring drift scores - high drift indicates execution diverged from original intent

faq.question: What is the Ambiguity threshold for seeding?
faq.answer: Ambiguity must be ≤ 0.2 before crystallizing into a seed. Calculate as 1 minus weighted clarity scores.

faq.question: How does Ralph mode differ from regular execution?
faq.answer: Ralph mode persists until verification passes or max iterations reached. It treats each failure as data for the next attempt - the boulder never stops.

faq.question: What does 3-stage evaluation check?
faq.answer: Mechanical (lint, build, tests), Semantic (acceptance criteria compliance), and Consensus (optional multi-model vote).

faq.question: What triggers Ralph mode?
faq.answer: Keywords: "ralph", "ooo", "don't stop", "must complete", "until it works", "keep going", "interview me", "stop prompting"

faq.question: How does drift detection work?
faq.answer: Drift score measures how far execution has deviated from the original specification. 0-0.15 is excellent, 0.15-0.30 is acceptable, 0.30+ needs correction.

faq.question: What platforms support Ralph?
faq.answer: Claude Code (native), Codex CLI (via setup script), Gemini CLI (AfterAgent hooks), and OpenCode (native skills).
